title: Message
subtitle: A parsed message in Io's AST. Carries a name, argument messages, the next message in the chain, and cached values for evaluation.

members.name: appendArg
members.description:
Adds aMessage to the argument list of receiver. Examples: 
    Io> message(a) appendArg(message(b))
    ==> a(b)

    Io> message(a(1,2)) appendArg(message(3))
    ==> a(1, 2, 3)

members.name: appendCachedArg
members.description: Adds aValue to the argument list of receiver as a cachedResult.

members.name: argAt
members.description: Returns Message object for the specified argument or Nil if none exists.

members.name: argCount
members.description:
Returns the number of arguments this message has. A faster way to do, msg arguments size. Examples, 
 Io> message(a(1,2,3)) argCount
    ==> 3

    Io> message(a) argCount
    ==> 0

members.name: argsEvaluatedIn
members.description:
Returns a List containing the argument messages evaluated in the
    context of anObject.

members.name: arguments
members.description:
Returns a list of the message objects that act as the
    receiver's arguments. Modifying this list will not alter the actual
    list of arguments. Use the arguments_() method to do that.

members.name: asMessageWithEvaluatedArgs
members.description:
Returns a copy of receiver with arguments evaluated in the context of
       sender if optionalContext is nil.

members.name: asSimpleString
members.description: Returns one-line string representation up to 40 characters long.

members.name: asStackEntry
members.description: Returns a string containing message name, file and line.

members.name: asString
members.description: Same as code().

members.name: cachedResult
members.description: Returns the cached result of the Message or Nil if there is none.

members.name: characterNumber
members.description:
Returns the message character number. The character number is typically
    the beginning character index in the source text from which the message was
    read.

members.name: clone
members.description: Returns a Message that is a deep copy of the receiver.

members.name: code
members.description:
Returns a String containing a decompiled code representation of the
    receiver.

members.name: codeOfLength
members.description: Same as <tt>Message code</tt>, but returns first <tt>n</tt> characters only.

members.name: description
members.description: Returns a string containing a short description of the method.

members.name: doInContext
members.description:
Evaluates the receiver in the context of anObject. Optional <tt>locals</tt>
    object is used as message sender. <tt>anObject</tt> is used as sender
    otherwise.

members.name: evaluatedArgs
members.description: Returns a List containing the argument messages evaluated in the context.

members.name: fromString
members.description:
Returns a new Message object for the compiled(but not executed)
    result of aString.

members.name: hasCachedResult
members.description: Returns true if there is a cached result. Nil is a valid cached result.

members.name: isEndOfLine
members.description: Returns true if the message marks the end of the line. A ';' message.

members.name: label
members.description:
Returns the message label. The label is typically set to the
    name of the file from which the source code for the message was read.

members.name: last
members.description: Returns the last message in the chain.

members.name: lastBeforeEndOfLine
members.description: Returns the last message in the chain before the EndOfLine or nil.

members.name: lineNumber
members.description:
Returns the line number of the message. The character number
    is typically the line number in the source text from which the message was
    read.

members.name: name
members.description: Returns the name of the receiver.

members.name: next
members.description:
Returns the next message in the message chain or nil if there is no next
    message.

members.name: nextIgnoreEndOfLines
members.description:
Returns the next message in the message chain which is not an
    EndOfLine or nil if there is no next message.

members.name: previous
members.description:
Returns the previous message in the message chain or Nil if there is no
    previous message.

members.name: removeCachedResult
members.description: Removes the cached result of the Message.

members.name: setArguments
members.description:
Sets the arguments of the receiver to deep copies of
    those contained in aListOfMessages.  Returns self.

members.name: setCachedResult
members.description: Sets the cached result of the message. Returns self.

members.name: setCharacterNumber
members.description: Sets the character number of the message. Returns self.

members.name: setLabel
members.description: Sets the label of the message and its children. Returns self.

members.name: setLineNumber
members.description: Sets the line number of the message. Returns self.

members.name: setName
members.description: Sets the name of the receiver. Returns self.

members.name: setNext
members.description:
Sets the next message in the message chain to a deep copy of
    aMessage or it removes the next message if aMessage is nil.

members.name: setPrevious
members.description:
Sets the previous message in the message chain to a deep copy of
    aMessage or it removes the previous message if aMessage is Nil.

members.name: union
members.signature: (other) Creates a union of the receiver and the other parameter.
members.description:
Returns a new message object with the receivers message as the first argument of the returned message, and the other parameter's arguments as each successive argument to the new message. 
  Io> message(a) union(message(b))
  ==> [unnamed](a, b)
